meaning: n.黑檀树,乌木,乌梅adj.乌木制的,黑檀的
examples: He had rich, soft ebony hair.
For her skin was Snow White, her cheeks rosy red, and her hair black as ebony.
But even in the absence of logging, the slow-growing ebony forests failed to thrive. Why?
In 2009, Ebony named him as one of the Ebony Power 150: the most influential Blacks in America.
Soon, King was off the sidewalk playing inside the clubs, blues palaces like Club Ebony.
